vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with the visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only a single, struggling, etiolated stem. P3 (Pothos) is present but severely compromised.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of silver guest)

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains dark, damp soil with no visible plant matter. Status: Failed.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains a single, thin, chlorotic stem with one small, pale leaf. Status: Critical/Terminal.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot with white rabbit anchor. The Pothos shows signs of severe light starvation and potential root rot. Status: Poor.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and saturated across all pots, suggesting poor drainage or over-watering.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P3/P4 area), likely an attempt at calcium supplementation or pest deterrent.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A small electronic component (IC chip) was present in early images and has been replaced by a charging cable in the current state.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, the Pothos (P3) has shown no new growth and maintains a downward, limp posture. The soil moisture levels appear static. The most significant change is the removal of the electronic component (IC chip) and the introduction of a white cable.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of decline. The lack of light (North window only) combined with saturated soil has led to a failure of the P1, P2, and P4 specimens. The remaining P3 specimen is exhibiting signs of severe stress (etiolation and lack of turgor pressure).
vision_report.anomalies: Presence of non-botanical debris (IC chip, cable, eggshells).
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit. First, I mapped the physical contents against the registry. Second, I validated the health of each specimen through pixel-level analysis of leaf turgor and soil saturation. The biome is currently failing; the plants are suffering from chronic light deficiency and likely root asphyxiation due to over-saturation. The 'New Intervention' of the cable suggests human activity, but the botanical health remains in a terminal trajectory.
